title: Translation workflow + first reviewed translation of the flagship guide (high-need language)
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task builds the translation workflow and delivers the first reviewer-approved translation of the flagship guide into a high-need language. Machine translation is a draft only; publication requires a qualified bilingual reviewer sign-off, and the translation must preserve citations, the not-advice framing, and the route-to-human pointers. The specific high-need language is TO BE SECURED (prioritized by need/take-up gap once the pilot jurisdiction is confirmed).
objective: Establish the translation workflow and deliver one reviewer-approved translation of the flagship guide into a high-need language, preserving citations, not-advice framing, and route-to-human pointers.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow allows machine translation as a draft only; publication requires a qualified bilingual reviewer sign-off (recorded in the per-language reviewer ledger)
The translated guide preserves citations, the not-advice framing, and the route-to-human pointers; benefits terminology checked by the bilingual reviewer
Benefits-accuracy of the translation confirmed against the source guide before publication
